label: EEG Studies
blurb: We place a comfortable cap with small sensors on your head to record your brain's electrical activity while you listen to stories, read sentences, or watch short cartoons. No needles, no discomfort — just your brain doing its thing!

label: Eye-Tracking While Reading
blurb: A small camera gently follows where your eyes move as you read sentences on a screen. Nothing touches you — we are just curious about how your eyes travel across text as you make sense of language.

label: Visual World Studies
blurb: Participants look at colorful pictures on a screen while listening to sentences. A camera tracks where their eyes go — no touching, no discomfort. This helps us understand how children and adults connect words to meaning in real time.

label: Language Tasks
blurb: Simple and fun activities — answering questions, playing word games, or working with a researcher on a computer task. There are no right or wrong answers; we are just curious how language works in your mind.
